title: 14-day transition from kibble to fresh food (cats)
summary: Cats are food-neophobic — slower transitions than dogs are essential. A rushed switch often ends in food refusal that can become dangerous (hepatic lipidosis if a cat skips meals for >24h). Stretch the transition to 14 days; longer if your cat is hesitant.

days.note: Tiny amount of fresh food, warm to body temperature. Some cats need this stage extended a full week.
Increase only if stool stays firm. Hold or step back if loose stools appear.
Half-and-half stage. Most cats stabilize here.
Almost there. Watch for food refusal — back off a day if needed.
Fresh food only. Add taurine supplement now if not already on it.

tips: Cats refusing food >24h can develop hepatic lipidosis. Never skip meals to force acceptance.
Warm the food slightly — cats prefer body-temperature food.
Sprinkle a small amount of nutritional yeast or freeze-dried treats on top to bridge flavor.
Old and young cats often need 21–28 days. Be patient.
Never mix taurine supplement into uneaten food — add it fresh each meal.
